name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: My opponent’s major endorsement.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
| Will you endorse my campaign today?
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=2502%2E35263%2E-oQVWn)
Peter — Earlier this month, Donald Trump came to Wisconsin and shook up this Senate race: He held a MAGA rally in Green Bay, personally attacked me from the stage, and even endorsed my Republican opponent, Eric Hovde. On policies from banning abortion to taking away health care and Social Security, you can’t tell Hovde apart from Trump. So Trump’s endorsement is the most coveted one a right-wing Republican like him can get. For me on the other hand, the most important endorsements I could receive are from hardworking folks like you.This incredible team of grassroots supporters helped me make history before, and you’ll be the reason I defeat my MAGA opponent in November. **I’m not asking you for a donation. Instead, I’m asking for your personal endorsement.** **[Will you help us make a statement by adding your name to endorse my reelection campaign today?](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/signup/2404_endorsetammy/?refcode=AU_STB_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20240426_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=2502%2E35263%2E-oQVWn)** | | [ENDORSE TAMMY](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/signup/2404_endorsetammy/?refcode=AU_STB_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20240426_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=2502%2E35263%2E-oQVWn)

— Tammy

name: Adam Frisch
subject: Fox News
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Adam for Colorado
body:
The day before the anniversary of the Supreme Court’s disastrous Dobbs decision, I woke up to Fox News and Lauren Boebert attacking my sister and my 81-year-old father.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/83804825/469559549/872526041?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjIxNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGVlMDIxMy0yOTA0LWVmMTEtOTZmMy03YzFlNTIxYjA3ZjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZbObor0H2J-fBgxIcYd-2wC11A0QgdtLhqjlmw3rlts=&emci=7af58d96-1e04-ef11-96f3-7c1e521b07f9&emdi=14ee0213-2904-ef11-96f3-7c1e521b07f9&ceid=19445147)
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/83804827/469559557/872526041?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjIxNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGVlMDIxMy0yOTA0LWVmMTEtOTZmMy03YzFlNTIxYjA3ZjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZbObor0H2J-fBgxIcYd-2wC11A0QgdtLhqjlmw3rlts=&emci=7af58d96-1e04-ef11-96f3-7c1e521b07f9&emdi=14ee0213-2904-ef11-96f3-7c1e521b07f9&ceid=19445147)
Hello,
The day before the anniversary of the Supreme Court’s disastrous _Dobbs_ decision, I woke up to Fox News and Lauren Boebert attacking my sister and my 81-year-old father.
For decades, Dad was an OB-GYN. He delivered thousands of babies and wrote books on how to get pregnant and to navigate menopause. Dad provided women with full access to all kinds of reproductive health care, including abortions, in the years right after _Roe_ – and he has now had to watch as women’s reproductive rights are gutted in the aftermath of _Dobbs_.
Dad and his clinic faced many threats over the years. It’s no exaggeration to say that he put his own safety – and that of his family – at risk in order to protect the rights of women to make their own medical decisions.
But I’m not sure he ever expected to see a sitting member of Congress attacking him – or my sister who followed in his footsteps – in a national news outlet. **It’s disgusting. These kinds of personal attacks against my family have no place in our politics and do nothing to serve the people of Western and Southern Colorado.**
If extremists like Lauren Boebert and my new Republican opponents want to have a conversation about reproductive rights in this country, I am more than happy to have that debate. We can talk about how I support _freedom_ and _liberty_ – and how they support big government intruding into the lives of women. We can talk about how they support a national ban on abortion – with no exceptions for rape, incest, or the life of the mother. And we can talk about how they are so extreme, they even want to ban the pill that’s been used by women for decades.
I am willing to talk all day about the fringe view that the government should be dictating women’s medical decisions. **But what I won’t accept is extremists like Lauren Boebert and Fox News attacking my father and my sister.**
I am _incredibly_ proud of my father. He provided health care to women when they needed it during some of the happiest – and most devastating – moments of their lives. Before retirement, he would drive two hours to Planned Parenthood and help teach the next generation of health care providers, like my sister. And no one – not Fox News, and especially not extremists like Lauren Boebert or my new Republican opponents – is going to stop my family from continuing to stand up for a woman’s right to choose.
**We already scared off Lauren Boebert. Last year, I came just 546 votes shy of defeating her and winning this seat. I’m ready to finish the job and replace Boebert in Congress in November, but these are the kinds of ugly attacks that I’m already up against.**
[**Please, if you can afford to, will you give $5 or more right now to my campaign to help me win this critical seat and replace Lauren Boebert in Congress? Our race was the closest in the entire country in 2022. But I’m not taking money from corporate PACs, so I’m counting on grassroots donors like you if I’m going to win.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/83804829/469559565/1610638071?refcode=em20240426pm-nd&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C35%2C50%2C100%2C250&refcode2=14ee0213-2904-ef11-96f3-7c1e521b07f9&recurring=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjIxNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGVlMDIxMy0yOTA0LWVmMTEtOTZmMy03YzFlNTIxYjA3ZjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ZbObor0H2J-fBgxIcYd-2wC11A0QgdtLhqjlmw3rlts=)

name: Josh Harder
subject: Why I'm stressed
body:
Hint: it's not our newborn.

Hi Peter, how are you?
I’ll admit, between welcoming our second daughter Karina and dealing with the MAGA extremists (who I’d argue act _worse_ than a baby), I’ve got my hands full.
But what’s increasing my stress levels the most is that Speaker Mike Johnson is ramping up his efforts to flip my seat red. He even held a fundraiser for my MAGA opponent in my district. In fact, we know from the FEC reports that the Speaker wrote a $60k check for my opponent.
There’s a lot of weight on my shoulders right now. Our democracy is on the line and now it feels like everything could come down to what happens in my race. But thankfully I have this grassroots team supporting me every step of the way.
**My team and I set a $15,000 end-of-month goal to respond to these latest developments, and I could really use your help to fight back.**
If you can chip in today, I’ll leave a link here: [_**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/JHCA-09-Email-April2024-FR-EOM**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/83805429/469564980/369747382?&refcode=E8208-20240426-EOM-SL-DeadlineEOM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTE0NGVkNTEtMmEwNC1lZjExLTk2ZjMtN2MxZTUyMWIwN2Y5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=TFDKcLL09oV8o_zy6f_AUDu1aahjN7cYXPZQO9jiSxA=)
Thank you,
Josh

Manteca, CA 95337

Fellow American,

It has been more than 50 years since New Jersey had a Republican Senator. _It is time to break the streak once and for all,**and I am the man for the job.**_ [**Are you with me?**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d559b1dc120c44c99ebfbdb52363c5a4&_e=Zcmyvo41XsFjWfopjAen61DqBGelF0faZmRtYlASzjkonCI3WX3aPj6sqrdMUZ8ZDgeMl7ZnoSSEji-BFzVmWKml7wsoBcv2tO-psGCj-eZ2EreYhvmJCIde5TRKv9NqfzhYzZRdtPNLPeV_zg9D4o5daof2Gns-Or2Hn0oZSxvwkyUQU5Z5HQOPlPMs9yeoiZuupjJELJYYbfezGpcCypcEyvavrLRgVGMfMFJ1NwYkKdy6kOSBK_ZwVaeZhYMSwvklES35y_q4S-FmBPK09Adg5sUhqXDhrzHJw_tbzbLsRpxw4Y-T6CXFQ0IydidsVA3X35m41wbraxvSplSI2RaJKonRUOCazcVQbCNqTb_hkBVlFIr1h9WE4Z_ADYGAHMffkRyi82qHrzwT4jMFPA%3D%3D)

[** FLIP NEW JERSEY RED **](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d559b1dc120c44c99ebfbdb52363c5a4&_e=Zcmyvo41XsFjWfopjAen61DqBGelF0faZmRtYlASzjkonCI3WX3aPj6sqrdMUZ8ZDgeMl7ZnoSSEji-BFzVmWKml7wsoBcv2tO-psGCj-eZ2EreYhvmJCIde5TRKv9NqfzhYzZRdtPNLPeV_zg9D4o5daof2Gns-Or2Hn0oZSxvwkyUQU5Z5HQOPlPMs9yeoiZuupjJELJYYbfezGpcCypcEyvavrLRgVGMfMFJ1NwYkKdy6kOSBK_ZwVaeZhYMS8sYNWk9cpBxKW4XIepoPyxSIh64F9qDDLLsBfL-2d7tzZtYfeZ4mYjAx5C0fvdUekNcCHv_thYjvgwEUtMIhckUOe59f2JCxVQ0LNP8lMN87ldztQqpJDLTkF15jQN4ObEhGfkctDEQQhLf1_gh9WQ%3D%3D)
I believe this race is a once-in-a-generation opportunity, and am ready to prove to the rest of the country that New Jersey is a lot more purple than people realize.

**I’m a lifelong Republican who stands strong in what I believe.** So, I’m standing up and being the change that I so greatly want to see.

**Washington doesn’t need another politician who heads to Capitol Hill just to go viral online and generate a million clicks.** If that’s what you’re looking for, _I’m not your guy._

_Washington doesn’t need another politician who is willing to discard their beliefs for whichever SuperPAC is writing the biggest check._

**Washington needs someone who can get people to work together to get things done for the American people. That’s what I’ve done for 35 years in business and that’s what I’ll do in Washington as New Jersey’s next Senator.**

We have a real opportunity here to make a change in New Jersey, but I know I can’t do it without a strong team around me, comprised of freedom-loving Americans just like yourself. [**Can I count on you to step off the sidelines and support me on this mission today?**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d559b1dc120c44c99ebfbdb52363c5a4&_e=Zcmyvo41XsFjWfopjAen61DqBGelF0faZmRtYlASzjkonCI3WX3aPj6sqrdMUZ8ZDgeMl7ZnoSSEji-BFzVmWKml7wsoBcv2tO-psGCj-eZ2EreYhvmJCIde5TRKv9NqfzhYzZRdtPNLPeV_zg9D4o5daof2Gns-Or2Hn0oZSxvwkyUQU5Z5HQOPlPMs9yeoiZuupjJELJYYbfezGpcCypcEyvavrLRgVGMfMFJ1NwYkKdy6kOSBK_ZwVaeZhYMSg1DKn8z_QE2eFcI8zdUBx22BFtQscxQEYC8BNqyDRTtLiFOobd9f3CB9pbZNdp-2nIpbPnmfthnPg0xeaOiwUAd_xA1hEGMyztLXovIlCkyu3u0Jn9X4ZdW4Qk8NpOzlC7cyZ_CbxUMz_rZHt7zdqA%3D%3D)

[** SUPPORT CURTIS BASHAW **](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d559b1dc120c44c99ebfbdb52363c5a4&_e=Zcmyvo41XsFjWfopjAen61DqBGelF0faZmRtYlASzjkonCI3WX3aPj6sqrdMUZ8ZDgeMl7ZnoSSEji-BFzVmWKml7wsoBcv2tO-psGCj-eZ2EreYhvmJCIde5TRKv9NqfzhYzZRdtPNLPeV_zg9D4o5daof2Gns-Or2Hn0oZSxvwkyUQU5Z5HQOPlPMs9yeoiZuupjJELJYYbfezGpcCypcEyvavrLRgVGMfMFJ1NwYkKdy6kOSBK_ZwVaeZhYMSCwu2cRsupV2uzcteRiAxE0HtUc8L3MyrNtt6xqzlohepzQr5Q4I_AgByjTBKKdZHZS7_ADDBmIX4qkDYin0TrFZLb4raRHcVyEunQoDcTXXxuCQ5BQ5h5B6oUn4cGpcQdYxi4oOti23PdrKZrFxigQ%3D%3D)

Gratefully,

[ ](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d559b1dc120c44c99ebfbdb52363c5a4&_e=Zcmyvo41XsFjWfopjAen61DqBGelF0faZmRtYlASzjkonCI3WX3aPj6sqrdMUZ8ZDgeMl7ZnoSSEji-BFzVmWKml7wsoBcv2tO-psGCj-eZ2EreYhvmJCIde5TRKv9NqfzhYzZRdtPNLPeV_zg9D4o5daof2Gns-Or2Hn0oZSxvwkyUQU5Z5HQOPlPMs9yeoiZuupjJELJYYbfezGpcCypcEyvavrLRgVGMfMFJ1NwYkKdy6kOSBK_ZwVaeZhYMSNOEEN0QfehrH_JEODN83DDlOvuQu4_rN47-D9AC4evJS1TON3LKqbkrpLvRkMCGVZlLJ83_MFlV40I0WEHBmqv8kAkw35kejB4JG1xDNKBU9ET0oeeIPm8lVIDlWn8OUpxXaRq3onUUP4ix1ooGUcQ%3D%3D)

Curtis Bashaw
Entrepreneur. Husband. Preservationist.

PS – if you want to volunteer your time to help me in my campaign efforts, give my team a shout here so we can be in further contact with you.

| **_GET TO KNOW CURTIS_**

**Curtis Bashaw is a hotel entrepreneur, job creator, and preservationist. He’s running for United States Senate to provide greater freedom and opportunities for all New Jerseyans.**

A lifelong New Jersey resident, Curtis grew up in Camden County and spent his summers at the Jersey Shore working as a bellhop and waiting tables at Congress Hall in Cape May. He has spent his life in the real estate and hospitality industries, and is the Founder and Managing Partner of Cape Resorts, a hotel company focusing on the restoration and operation of classic American resort properties in New Jersey and New York.

Throughout his career, Curtis has worked to build communities and bring people together. He is widely recognized for leading the landmark restorations of Congress Hall and the Virginia Hotel in Cape May, and for launching creative programming to dramatically extend Cape May’s season, a catalyst for the economic rebirth of one of the Jersey Shore’s most beautiful communities.

Curtis is a successful businessman and job creator, employing more than 1,000 people in New Jersey and New York. Curtis’ company owns and operates eight hotels, ten cottages, six restaurants, four retail outlets and a working farm, all on the Jersey Shore and Long Island’s East End. Curtis lives in Cape May with his husband, Will.

| [** Stand with Curtis Bashaw! **](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d559b1dc120c44c99ebfbdb52363c5a4&_e=Zcmyvo41XsFjWfopjAen61DqBGelF0faZmRtYlASzjkonCI3WX3aPj6sqrdMUZ8ZDgeMl7ZnoSSEji-BFzVmWKml7wsoBcv2tO-psGCj-eZ2EreYhvmJCIde5TRKv9NqfzhYzZRdtPNLPeV_zg9D4o5daof2Gns-Or2Hn0oZSxvwkyUQU5Z5HQOPlPMs9yeoiZuupjJELJYYbfezGpcCypcEyvavrLRgVGMfMFJ1NwYkKdy6kOSBK_ZwVaeZhYMS1eHORPPrqD0mUl2PzZjH1AL0MxbODPyp44o1V4eqeMtNeVq5lxbOn6JbC8An2lURiQGvJ8vtCAmvwk54vEB-D0izbTbUP4I9tdrRH7Ja7LTSGH6QAH_yVRuP_A4_NWePlcKS-lkHVgPqm5nexqk5YA%3D%3D)

body:
| | | [](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=91cf6dcd7c93479e9d0607c388ae03fb&_e=_KrB2kX-5hLuf69BmMU3mWVUnhwZHoOp07OVggBfkeRMKkt8kQIC_5cjpn13ya-jhu-2ZpmvSiwl5crnEaF80aAM5z8hludCd8LPvUkntXoRa4aiDuyUEx6LYDqDHp3AKd5iBohrtIfK1Bqd4rgKhzz11UCnlx4Eox2t_eyHV1g3wzm1rLmNeMPVd1ec6aXySZ1Gk8KLh4UvalALLhcLC7Qxg_0qliLsx_4O7YKge9_EOzIOOTD3gSMHvfuBLWwub7KTD1F0KLJ8NNo0u7r4cxnLYaT1jj1qBxItyIyWqlSI7v6mnSqu5rx8ScYF5ik9BPodw8bFlRxA0Pl1LS1RxDP5YUu69MeUrkkF9fz6fHGcv9Z7Rq6GgMyHud4HnKei)

Peter,

God. Family. Country.

As a Missourian, those are the values that guide me, and I know they guide my neighbors and my community too.

But Democrats want to ERASE those values and turn Missouri into a woke experiment state like California or New York.

**My Democrat opponent is one of the biggest fundraisers in the country, and he’ll stop at _NOTHING_ to make us the next failed blue state. **

I love Missouri too much to let that happen.

**[I’m counting on my grassroots patriot team to help me DEFEND our state.](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=91cf6dcd7c93479e9d0607c388ae03fb&_e=_KrB2kX-5hLuf69BmMU3mWVUnhwZHoOp07OVggBfkeRMKkt8kQIC_5cjpn13ya-jhu-2ZpmvSiwl5crnEaF80aAM5z8hludCd8LPvUkntXoRa4aiDuyUEx6LYDqDHp3AKd5iBohrtIfK1Bqd4rgKhzz11UCnlx4Eox2t_eyHV1g3wzm1rLmNeMPVd1ec6aXySZ1Gk8KLh4UvalALLhcLC7Qxg_0qliLsx_4O7YKge98rjZKjorm4r5de9v4x4sGJFwqwn9Rpgg5piDFpf1QzJ0pKgxfo_8GNJdeqQP9ghfTGbei-f6HtLuF6W6DCc7Y3QW-w13lfzz7fk6WDYJsPDldoOZyENL7eouskRBybaJMUmWupIsrlqih_RQNTvrz6)**

---
[**TAKE A STAND WITH JOSH**](http://email.hawleyformo.com/u/click?_t=11a592fdc01846d4a1c728421295853d&_m=91cf6dcd7c93479e9d0607c388ae03fb&_e=_KrB2kX-5hLuf69BmMU3mWVUnhwZHoOp07OVggBfkeRMKkt8kQIC_5cjpn13ya-jhu-2ZpmvSiwl5crnEaF80aAM5z8hludCd8LPvUkntXoRa4aiDuyUEx6LYDqDHp3AKd5iBohrtIfK1Bqd4rgKhzz11UCnlx4Eox2t_eyHV1g3wzm1rLmNeMPVd1ec6aXySZ1Gk8KLh4UvalALLhcLC7Qxg_0qliLsx_4O7YKge9_XNCxN9fCkIYQPS9xZSpULfM39XEYBJ0lytVKEnhnbM8xfkq9YlpoHmTvOIfyPr8NEdC8y7ifyl5FgCvTSS6M0vJysy2kopq0o92o34H4CxXNpWmcJPgQ6spv-T6ho2axBUNanI-bGG0qe11twzGp9)
---
Here in Missouri, it doesn’t matter the color of your skin or where you come from. If you work hard and obey the law, anyone can succeed - and that makes the radical Left FURIOUS.

They want to replace our meritocracy with woke DEI failure and poison every institution in our state from our courts to our schools.

_Aloha Peter, this is Mazie. My friend and colleague in the Senate, Bob Casey, is now headed to the general election in one of the most competitive races this year. We must win his seat to protect our Democratic Senate majority, so please read his message and split a donation between our campaigns if you can._
* * *
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/83805706/469579495/1468470916?refcode=AU_MKH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20240426_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MTU3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMjM1NDVhYS0yZTA0LWVmMTEtOTZmMy03YzFlNTIxYjA3ZjkiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WouLjV2p5bm6Gw_LgyFFeSnMY11AiGSLQWB0317HfeM=) Peter, With the primary now behind us, I wanted to take a moment to say thank you to you, Peter. If I’m going to continue working to lower costs for working families, build a stronger economy that works for everyone, and ensure that our seniors and veterans have the benefits they've earned, I need you behind me **every step of the way.**

If I’m going to continue working to lower costs for working families, build a stronger economy that works for everyone, and ensure that our seniors and veterans have the benefits they've earned, I need you behind me every step of the way. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Mazie Hirono and Bob Casey will go through immediately: | = = | = = | = = With gratitude, Bob Casey Friends of Mazie Hirono 335 Merchant Street #677 Honolulu, HI 96809 United States mailto:info@mazieforhawaii.com | Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii

subject: We could get there with your support

body:
Following up from earlier this week
| | |
---
Friend, earlier this week our grassroots team reached out about Debbie's FINAL $600,000 fundraising goal before the end of the month.

We were worried because we were pacing way behind just as polling showed Debbie _leading_ Rick Scott in this race that could decide Senate control.

**But now, we're _so close_ to hitting this pivotal goal: If 11 more supporters from your area chipped in just $5, our calculations show we'd hit our final goal before our end of month deadline.**

Debbie is counting on your grassroots support to defeat Rick Scott and save our Senate majority. Please, chip in now using this link: [**https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/mw_dmp_2024_email_fr**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/83805758/469584234/1820369458?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&refcode=jtk1138-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGVkZGRjMTItMzAwNC1lZjExLTk2ZjMtN2MxZTUyMWIwN2Y5IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=q-HjvOiZ2WY5SI9QW8j4T2G6G8uJXY0AvvaQE6GRbyo=)

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Trump Persecution Update
body:
Court Rejects Biden DOJ Stonewall!

[INSIDE JW]

Judge Stops Justice Department Stonewalling in Releasing Biden
Interview Recordings

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32277&F=T&&hash=e9a9176a100a9f43360230adcc0f28fa7fc3b3cbdd9b58e04e1de11d5e7a2b98]
We’re pleased to report that a federal judge has blocked
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32278&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1leHRlbnNpb24tZGVuaWFsLTAwNzAwLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=0617b21215660785ae5303973c63b51a69733e6acf8ffddae9a039d4827ab438]
the Justice Department’s effort to delay producing recordings of
President Biden’s interview with Special Counsel Robert Hur.

The Biden Justice Department was trying to help Joe Biden politically
by hiding the recordings of his special counsel interviews. These
recordings are essential to the public interest in obtaining
information about any presidential misconduct, crimes, and cognitive
challenges.

The Justice Department was supposed to let us and the court know its
position on releasing the recordings on April 30. However, the agency
asked the court for at least one more month (May 27 or later) to state
its position on whether it is going to release any recordings.

We filed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32279&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1leHRlbnNpb24tb3Bwby0wMDcwMC9dYW4/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=bd5302ff870937249dbc9cfeb7230b1d169ced5857279159680f05ecaa42fc0c
opposition
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32266&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1leHRlbnNpb24tb3Bwby0wMDcwMC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=84e6b16438c40cf7a0fd1af159cf4e2f927df6b7e452557af93ce89caddcf058]
to the department’s request, and Judge Timothy Kelly of the U.S.
District Court for the District of Columbia agreed with us.

Here’s the background. On March 11, 2024, we filed a Freedom of
Information Act (FOIA)
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32280&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYmlkZW4tc3BlY2lhbC1jb3Vuc2VsLWludGVydmlld3MvXWxhd3N1aXQ/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=006be3f2a0ec76d4758594875a1eca36a12e42fa57f45aefd54f6d8919315677
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=31590&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYmlkZW4tc3BlY2lhbC1jb3Vuc2VsLWludGVydmlld3MvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=5e35eab9c3bd168fe398125086a7964bc0469879f3e6fddc4d4dad65680cb648]
against the U.S. Department of Justice in the U.S. District Court for
the District of Columbia after Justice failed to respond to a February
2024 FOIA request for records of all special counsel interviews of
President Biden (_Judicial Watch, Inc. v. U.S. Department of Justice_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=31585&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1pbnRlcnZpZXctY29tcGxhaW50LTAwNzAwLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=6dc84b815e33e4d79cc93a6ce0254c139b20bb5b159e92efdbcbf14e22ef5017]
(No. 1:24-cv-00700)). A redacted transcript of the Biden interview was
released on
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32281&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1wYXJ0aWFsLXRyYW5zY3JpcHQtMDA3MDAvXUFwcmlsP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=ff2d26262fb0addc3a580ee5e52b8106e63662b88a9124b5d7a2df6bb3ef3f99
15
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32267&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1wYXJ0aWFsLXRyYW5zY3JpcHQtMDA3MDAvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=442220348312d5842055bdaeb2830aeeef23d2365c4d89c61925a0db447ba364].

On February 5, 2024, Special Counsel Robert Hur
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=31638&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3Yvc3RvcmFnZS9yZXBvcnQtZnJvbS1zcGVjaWFsLWNvdW5zZWwtcm9iZXJ0LWstaHVyLWZlYnJ1YXJ5LTIwMjQucGRmXWlzc3VlZD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=75c2f96a9b471b790f07fcd1bb51c4c28db92b2dbb052e7515f48e7def650081
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=31466&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3Yvc3RvcmFnZS9yZXBvcnQtZnJvbS1zcGVjaWFsLWNvdW5zZWwtcm9iZXJ0LWstaHVyLWZlYnJ1YXJ5LTIwMjQucGRmP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=06a6d548e618f1e7fc59649f0b7384a7c832a2ab167880afc3f21250cdc0d0d0]
the “Report of the Special Counsel on the Investigation Into
Unauthorized Removal, Retention, and Disclosure of Classified
Documents Discovered at Locations Including the Penn Biden Center and
the Delaware Private Residence of President Joseph R. Biden, Jr.”

In the report, Hur called Biden a “well-meaning, elderly man with a
poor memory” and declined to charge Biden with a “serious
felony:”

We have also considered that, at trial, Mr. Biden would likely present
himself to a jury, as he did during our interview of him, as a
sympathetic, well-meaning, elderly man with a poor memory. Based on
our direct interactions with and observations of him, he is someone
for whom many jurors will want to identify reasonable doubt. It would
be difficult to convince a jury that they should convict him-by then a
former president well into his eighties-of a serious felony that
requires a mental state of willfulness.

Prior to the finalization of the report, the White House issued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=31639&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3Yvc3RvcmFnZS9yZXBvcnQtZnJvbS1zcGVjaWFsLWNvdW5zZWwtcm9iZXJ0LWstaHVyLWZlYnJ1YXJ5LTIwMjQucGRmXWE/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=4fd535d6a2f73b807fc99feb4ccf301e626b546d52512a1cb9b380d7942e1147
letter
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=31466&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3Yvc3RvcmFnZS9yZXBvcnQtZnJvbS1zcGVjaWFsLWNvdW5zZWwtcm9iZXJ0LWstaHVyLWZlYnJ1YXJ5LTIwMjQucGRmP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=06a6d548e618f1e7fc59649f0b7384a7c832a2ab167880afc3f21250cdc0d0d0]
to the Special Counsel’s office attacking the report’s
“treatment of President Biden’s memory,” and added “there is
ample evidence from your interview that the President did well in
answering your questions ...”

We
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32282&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1leHRlbnNpb24tb3Bwby0wMDcwMC9dYXJndWU/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=b6fb65b1690e7b0843b1fa8314638ca3b5a18eebb7325cd04b27c0cd23fff116
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32268&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZG9qLWJpZGVuLWh1ci1leHRlbnNpb24tb3Bwby0wMDcwMC9dOj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=af419f26103b42e5360debe5de1c79884c66e96ef918e3bc3dca4fd1499eae04

> This case could not be any more straightforward, and [the Justice
> Department] cannot and does not provide any substantive reasons why
> an extension of time is necessary at this time.... Judicial Watch,
> along with other media organizations as well as Congressional
> committees, have sought these materials to enable the public to form
> its own conclusions about the Special Counsel’s characterizations
> of President Biden’s testimony.

***

> [The Justice Department] can alleviate any administrative burden by
> informing Judicial Watch and the Court whether it intends to produce
> the recordings. If it produces the recordings, the case is over.
(We have several ongoing FOIA lawsuits about Biden’s document
scandals and the related unprecedented partisan prosecutorial and
judicial abuses of former President Donald J. Trump.)

I’ll be sure to report back to you if we hear any news of the
release of the Biden audio (or video) recordings!

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES OVER TERMINATION OF PRESIDENT TRUMP’S SECURITY
CLEARANCE

Biden’s lieutenants in the federal bureaucracy are brazen in their
efforts to get Trump.

Judicial Watch just filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA)

retroactively after his June 2023

(No. 9:23-cr-80101)).

We filed the lawsuit after the Energy Department failed to comply with
a January 18, 2024, FOIA request for its records and communications
concerning retroactively terminating Trump’s security clearance
and/or access to classified information.

In our lawsuit we point to the February 2024
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32285&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY291cnRsaXN0ZW5lci5jb20vZG9ja2V0LzY3NDkwMDcwLzI3Ny91bml0ZWQtc3RhdGVzLXYtdHJ1bXAvXXJlc3BvbnNlP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=b59d166e02ce91754f90198e8894e1c46b52df5864afd541c6fbad45ba6b1ce3

to Trump’s January 2024 motion in which Smith acknowledges the
existence of a June 2023 memorandum prepared by an Energy Department
official regarding the security clearance.

The Special Counsel’s office describes the memorandum’s contents
and asserts that it had produced the record to Trump. Smith also
acknowledges requesting and receiving additional “responsive”
records from the Energy Department, including “approximately 30
pages of records and eight emails.” Smith asserts that he was “now
producing” the 30 pages to Trump and withholding the eight emails.

Trump’s lawyers suggest in the January 2024
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32286&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZG9jdW1lbnRjbG91ZC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzLzI0MzYyNzEwLXRydW1wLW1vdGlvbi10by1jb21wZWwtZGlzY292ZXJ5LWZsb3JpZGEtY2FzZV1tb3Rpb24/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=1ae6ce5566c6c398aaed29a962f5a8aa30cfab7adeff6d4dc0537fbc4b76a684

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32237&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZG9jdW1lbnRjbG91ZC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzLzI0MzYyNzEwLXRydW1wLW1vdGlvbi10by1jb21wZWwtZGlzY292ZXJ5LWZsb3JpZGEtY2FzZT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=4a822b3cb59ba6713c80a2366c04b67f2e5f69444b2ae4436a1f956829eb53a7]
that Trump had a high-level security clearance as recently as 2023.

Lawyers for Trump say a government document from June 2023 still
listed him with a “Q” clearance from the Energy Department. The
document was dated a few weeks after prosecutors indicted Trump in the
classified documents case. A “Q” clearance refers to a type of
security clearance handled by the Department of Energy, which holds
classified information focused largely on nuclear secrets.

It looks like the Department of Energy is trying to manufacture a
criminal case. What are they hiding?

We are in the forefront of court battles for transparency in the Biden
administration’s targeting of Trump.

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32272&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdHJ1bXAtdHJpYWwtcHJvc2VjdXRvcnMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=5c3aa6a1d08e5c3aed18ea38ee06c36dca3e05a99b545cf9a900aabcd827d172]
the silly
case brought in Manhattan against Donald Trump by DA Alvin Bragg. From
our latest _Investigative Bulletin_:

> The Donald Trump business records trial opened yesterday in New York
> City with prosecutors signaling a wide-ranging case.
> Documents—business records—related to payments from the Trump
> Organization to an adult film star showed “election fraud” and a
> “criminal conspiracy and cover up” designed to prevent
> information about an alleged affair from emerging before the 2016
> presidential election, prosecutor Matthew Colangelo said.
>
> At the heart of the case are thirty-four counts
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=29794&F=T&&hash=88415f93d20a54ba6490e747c3ba0f8b3c616f48b4e720e69acb23d3780f8690]
of
> falsifying business records—generally misdemeanor charges that in
> this case Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg is attempting to elevate to
> felonies. Trump defense attorney Todd Blanche told the jury that the
> thirty-four Bragg charges signified no crimes—that Trump was just
> doing what a business leader does: sign papers prepared by his
> office.
>
> “The thirty-four counts are really just thirty-four pieces of
> paper.” Blanche said. Regarding the paper trail offered up by the
> prosecution, Blanche said that Trump “had nothing to do with the
> invoice, with the check being generated or with the entry on the
> ledger.” Blanche also attacked the prosecution’s contention
> that the payments were attempts to influence the 2016 election. “I
> have a spoiler alert,” he said. “There’s nothing wrong with
> trying to influence an election. It’s called democracy.”
>
> Prosecutors face two big problems with their case: a deeply flawed
> key witness and a largely untested legal ploy to leverage
> misdemeanor business-records charges into felony-level convictions.
>
> The case centers around former Trump Organization executive turned
> Trump nemesis, Michael Cohen. A self-proclaimed former Trump
> “fixer,” Cohen has a long criminal record and well-known hatred
> for the forty-fifth president of the United States. In 2016, Cohen
> expected a position in the Trump Administration, which was not
> forthcoming. Cohen later became a frequent guest on MSNBC, railing
> against Trump, and in 2022 he published a three-hundred-page
> diatribe against Trump, “Revenge
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32248&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cua2lya3VzcmV2aWV3cy5jb20vYm9vay1yZXZpZXdzL21pY2hhZWwtY29oZW4vcmV2ZW5nZS1jb2hlbi9dLuKAnT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=c01ae20c2a02239287d115f307683f5dcccdd0ba3ad105d41e7d84827a8419fe
>
> In 2018, Cohen pleaded guilty in federal court
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32249&F=T&&hash=746eda26312c4d9d992fcdd8c93449614fd59f6c57f767af3c9acedddd061995]
to
> tax evasion, campaign finance violations related to the current New
> York case, and false statements. He was sentenced to a three-year
> prison term. Later that year, he was back in federal court to plead
> guilty to lying to Congress.
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32250&F=T&&hash=98d311330ad9549c627b46117a8f7c314458d65cc7bb9adeb07a7927ac3b396a.]
>
> A month ago, a judge denied Cohen’s request for an early end to
> his supervised release from jail time, saying Cohen had
> likely “committed perjury”
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32250&F=T&&hash=98d311330ad9549c627b46117a8f7c314458d65cc7bb9adeb07a7927ac3b396a.]
in
> past testimony—a finding brushed aside by the judges in Trump’s
> current civil and criminal cases in New York.
>
> Much of the current case will hinge on Cohen’s credibility on the
> stand. It’s worth noting that both federal authorities and
> Bragg’s predecessor as Manhattan DA, Cy Vance, declined to bring
> the business-records case, uneasy with Cohen and a strategy for
> getting the misdemeanor charges to felony-level crimes. Mark
> Pomerantz, a senior Vance prosecutor who resigned after differences
> with Bragg on Trump prosecutions, noted in his book, “People vs.
> Donald Trump,” that while Cohen could be charming and credible, he
> also was “a somewhat feral creature.” Cohen, Pomerantz wrote,
> has a “penchant for publicity, exaggeration, and grandiose
> statements” that turns people off.
>
> The second big problem for prosecutors are those thirty-four
> business records charges usually clocking in as misdemeanors. In
> opening statements, the prosecution indicated it could prove the
> charges were felonies by showing they were connected to “election
> fraud.” But the statute prosecutors rely on has nothing to do with
> election law. New York state law (Section 175.10
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32251&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly95cGRjcmltZS5jb20vcGVuYWwubGF3L2FydGljbGUxNzUucGhwI3AxNzUuMDU/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=c160df02697c979baa3c46fe5aeafcb36886194fc9dad002cda087229cbbf377]
of the
> penal code) moves falsification of business records from misdemeanor
> to felony if the defendant’s “intent to defraud includes an
> intent to commit another crime.”
>
> What precisely is that other crime? At the moment, prosecutors
> aren’t saying. But eventually they’ll have to come up with
> something specific.
>
> With a jury drawn from deep blue Manhattan, it may not matter.
> Jurors may simply dislike Trump so much that they’ll look for any
> path to conviction. But juries are unpredictable. And the view from
> here is that Michael Cohen could emerge as a big liability. That
> sketchy ploy to elevate misdemeanors into felonies also gives the
> Trump team strong grounds for appeal.
>
BIDEN AGENCY SUES REPUBLICAN DONOR’S BUSINESS FOR RACIAL

The Biden administration continues to use its weaponized government

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32288&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZ292dC1zdWVzLXJlcHVibGljYW4tZG9ub3JzLWJpei9dOj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=9422533c41a89e112e80206daf9d1a33eeab2e60a44d5ddd29c99ab5d9d2f068

> The Biden administration appears to be using a federal agency to go
> after a family that has donated significantly to Republicans by
> suing its company for “racially discriminatory hiring practice”
> over background checks. The business, Sheetz Inc., is a chain of
> convenience stores and the Sheetz family has long supported
> Republicans in Pennsylvania and the Midwest, according to records
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32289&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3NoZWV0ei10YWJsZS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=f009b858e0f9730a408cb22b645973f004c1877271ca23cb433bd8ae3eebb033]
obtained by
> Judicial Watch. In the last few years, the Sheetz’s have donated
> hundreds of thousands of dollars to Republican causes and political
> candidates, including presidential candidate Mitt Romney,
> Pennsylvania Senate candidate Dave McCormick and the National
> Republican Congressional Committee. The most recent donation of
> $24,500 by Chairman Steve Sheetz was made a few months ago to
> McCormick, a West Point graduate and combat veteran endorsed by
> former President Donald Trump.
>
> Now the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC), which is
> charged with enforcing the nation’s workplace discrimination laws,
> is suing Sheetz Inc. for “racially discriminatory hiring practice
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32290&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZWVvYy5nb3YvbmV3c3Jvb20vZWVvYy1zdWVzLXNoZWV0ei1pbmMtcmFjaWFsbHktZGlzY3JpbWluYXRvcnktaGlyaW5nLXByYWN0aWNlXeKAnT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=fb87b7119fe400f9d9447abed600b0c6ef7aa78d82077ace31fcac43b8ec8d79
> over background checks. The agency charges that the company’s
> criminal history screening causes discriminatory impact against
> black, native American and other workers. In the lawsuit the Biden
> administration writes that Sheetz has maintained a longstanding
> practice of screening all job applicants for records of criminal
> conviction and then denying them employment based on those records.
> The company operates stores in over 600 locations throughout six
> states. The EEOC charges that the background checks
> disproportionately screened out black, native American/Alaska native
> and multiracial applicants. “Sheetz’s company-wide hiring
> practices violated provisions of Title VII that prohibit disparate
> impact discrimination,” according to the agency.
>
> In its complaint, which was filed in Maryland, the EEOC does not
> allege that Sheetz was motivated by race when making hiring
> decisions. The criminal screenings nevertheless resulted in racial
> discrimination, which violates federal law prohibiting facially
> neutral employment practices that cause a discriminatory impact
> because of race when those practices are not job-related and
> consistent with business necessity or where alternative practices
> with less discriminatory impact are available. “Federal law
> mandates that employment practices causing a disparate impact
> because of race or other protected classifications must be shown by
> the employer to be necessary to ensure the safe and efficient
> performance of the particular jobs at issue,” said EEOC Regional
> Attorney Debra M. Lawrence. “Even when such necessity is proven,
> the practice remains unlawful if there is an alternative practice
> available that is comparably effective in achieving the employer’s
> goals but causes less discriminatory effect.” An EEOC director
> stresses the agency’s commitment to reintegrating individuals with
> criminal records into society by ensuring they have fair access to
> employment and other essential services.
>
> Last fall the EEOC directed
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=30309&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZ292dC1oaXJlcy1tb3JlLWNyaW1pbmFscy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=97264a8e9c1dbc9f64533ace43a1178a8dfbee6af29bc03fa485c87888c8c429]
> government agencies to “widely publicize” they are “hiring
> persons with criminal conduct issues in their background checks”
> as part of a Biden executive order
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=68106&L=32291&F=T&&hash=e5b56868f39f5125c183662cb67fb4d05ea76d111ad5d189f56de579573a6f1c]
> requiring diversity, equity and inclusion in the federal workforce
> by, among other things, expanding employment opportunities for
> convicted individuals. Job applicants with criminal records are
> rarely eliminated from government jobs since the president issued
> the directive, the EEOC conceded at the time, but agency leaders
> believe more must be done to accommodate them and, when conducting
> background checks, the agency says employers should take a holistic
> approach with consideration for mitigating circumstances. In two
> reports issued last year, the EEOC explained that before Biden’s
> order an agency task force charged with identifying vulnerable
> workers and finding ways to better serve them classified “formerly
> incarcerated persons as one category of vulnerable workers due to
> the challenges they face in securing employment after their
> incarceration.” Years ago, the agency also made background checks
> related to arrest and conviction records among its “national
> substantive area priorities because African Americans and Latinos
> are disproportionately incarcerated.” Now the taxpayer-funded
> federal agency is going after businesses that screen employees with
> background checks, spending public resources to sue them.

> to purchase content or pay dues to NPR. “It is bad enough that so
> many media outlets push their slanted views instead of reporting the
> news, but it is even more egregious for hardworking taxpayers to be
> forced to pay for it,” said Congressman Bob Good, the lawmaker
> behind the measure. “My legislation would ensure no taxpayer
> dollars are used to fund the woke, leftist propaganda of National
> Public Radio.”

Until next week,

clean_body: Court Rejects Biden DOJ Stonewall! [INSIDE JW] Judge Stops Justice Department Stonewalling in Releasing Biden Interview Recordings [] We’re pleased to report that a federal judge has blocked [] the Justice Department’s effort to delay producing recordings of President Biden’s interview with Special Counsel Robert Hur. The Biden Justice Department was trying to help Joe Biden politically by hiding the recordings of his special counsel interviews. These recordings are essential to the public interest in obtaining information about any presidential misconduct, crimes, and cognitive challenges. The Justice Department was supposed to let us and the court know its position on releasing the recordings on April 30. However, the agency asked the court for at least one more month (May 27 or later) to state its position on whether it is going to release any recordings. We filed [ opposition [] to the department’s request, and Judge Timothy Kelly of the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia agreed with us. Here’s the background. On March 11, 2024, we filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) [ [] against the U.S. Department of Justice in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after Justice failed to respond to a February 2024 FOIA request for records of all special counsel interviews of President Biden (Judicial Watch, Inc. v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 1:24-cv-00700)). A redacted transcript of the Biden interview was released on [ 15 []. On February 5, 2024, Special Counsel Robert Hur [ [] the “Report of the Special Counsel on the Investigation Into Unauthorized Removal, Retention, and Disclosure of Classified Documents Discovered at Locations Including the Penn Biden Center and the Delaware Private Residence of President Joseph R. Biden, Jr.” In the report, Hur called Biden a “well-meaning, elderly man with a poor memory” and declined to charge Biden with a “serious felony:” We have also considered that, at trial, Mr. Biden would likely present himself to a jury, as he did during our interview of him, as a sympathetic, well-meaning, elderly man with a poor memory. Based on our direct interactions with and observations of him, he is someone for whom many jurors will want to identify reasonable doubt. It would be difficult to convince a jury that they should convict him-by then a former president well into his eighties-of a serious felony that requires a mental state of willfulness. Prior to the finalization of the report, the White House issued [ letter [] to the Special Counsel’s office attacking the report’s “treatment of President Biden’s memory,” and added “there is ample evidence from your interview that the President did well in answering your questions ...” We [ [ This case could not be any more straightforward, and [the Justice Department] cannot and does not provide any substantive reasons why an extension of time is necessary at this time.... Judicial Watch, along with other media organizations as well as Congressional committees, have sought these materials to enable the public to form its own conclusions about the Special Counsel’s characterizations of President Biden’s testimony. [The Justice Department] can alleviate any administrative burden by informing Judicial Watch and the Court whether it intends to produce the recordings. If it produces the recordings, the case is over. (We have several ongoing FOIA lawsuits about Biden’s document scandals and the related unprecedented partisan prosecutorial and judicial abuses of former President Donald J. Trump.) I’ll be sure to report back to you if we hear any news of the release of the Biden audio (or video) recordings! JUDICIAL WATCH SUES OVER TERMINATION OF PRESIDENT TRUMP’S SECURITY CLEARANCE Biden’s lieutenants in the federal bureaucracy are brazen in their efforts to get Trump. Judicial Watch just filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) [ [] against the U.S. Department of Energy for records about the retroactive termination of former President Donald Trump’s security clearance and/or access to classified information (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Energy [] (No. 1:24-cv-00744)). We cite Trump’s January 12, 2024, motion to compel discovery in his criminal prosecution in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Florida, in which the former president asserts that Department of Energy attempted to terminate his security clearance retroactively after his June 2023 [ [] by Special Counsel Jack Smith (United States v. Trump, et al., [] (No. 9:23-cr-80101)). We filed the lawsuit after the Energy Department failed to comply with a January 18, 2024, FOIA request for its records and communications concerning retroactively terminating Trump’s security clearance and/or access to classified information. In our lawsuit we point to the February 2024 [ [] to Trump’s January 2024 motion in which Smith acknowledges the existence of a June 2023 memorandum prepared by an Energy Department official regarding the security clearance. The Special Counsel’s office describes the memorandum’s contents and asserts that it had produced the record to Trump. Smith also acknowledges requesting and receiving additional “responsive” records from the Energy Department, including “approximately 30 pages of records and eight emails.” Smith asserts that he was “now producing” the 30 pages to Trump and withholding the eight emails. Trump’s lawyers suggest in the January 2024 [ to compel discovery [] that Trump had a high-level security clearance as recently as 2023. Lawyers for Trump say a government document from June 2023 still listed him with a “Q” clearance from the Energy Department. The document was dated a few weeks after prosecutors indicted Trump in the classified documents case. A “Q” clearance refers to a type of security clearance handled by the Department of Energy, which holds classified information focused largely on nuclear secrets. It looks like the Department of Energy is trying to manufacture a criminal case. What are they hiding? We are in the forefront of court battles for transparency in the Biden administration’s targeting of Trump. In August 2023, we filed a [ [] against the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA) for records of the Archives’ role in President Trump’s White House records controversy; whether it offered Trump a secure storage location other than the National Archives; and if the Archives consulted with the Office of the Director of National Intelligence regarding the classification or declassification procedures of any of the alleged classified documents found at Trump’s Florida residence. In June 2023, we obtained DOJ records [] that showed top officials of the National Security Division discussing the political implications of Trump allowing CNN to use closed-circuit TV (CCTV) footage of the raid on his Mar-a-Lago home. The documents confirmed that the Justice Department had asked that Mar-a-Lago CCTV be turned off before the raid. A separate Judicial Watch FOIA lawsuit [] against the National Archives and Records Administration resulted in the release of records about the unprecedented document dispute between Archives and President Trump. Click here [] or here [] to review the records. In August 2022, we successfully [ to unseal [] the search warrant affidavit [] used to justify the unprecedented raid on the home of former President Trump. In September 2022, we [ [] lawsuits against the DOJ for its records and the FBI’s records about the Mar-a-Lago raid search warrant application and approval, as well as communications about the warrant between the FBI, Executive Office of the President and the Secret Service. In October 2022, we [ [] the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) for all communications of the U.S. Secret Service internally and with the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) regarding the raid on Trump’s home and for any video or audio recordings made during the raid. In November 2022, we [ [] the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) for all communications between the Secret Service and Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) regarding the search warrant that precipitated the raid on former Trump’s Florida residence at Mar-a-Lago. TRUMP TRIAL PROSECUTORS FACE BIG PROBLEMS Micah Morrison, our chief investigative reporter, takes [ [] the silly case brought in Manhattan against Donald Trump by DA Alvin Bragg. From our latest Investigative Bulletin: The Donald Trump business records trial opened yesterday in New York City with prosecutors signaling a wide-ranging case. Documents—business records—related to payments from the Trump Organization to an adult film star showed “election fraud” and a “criminal conspiracy and cover up” designed to prevent information about an alleged affair from emerging before the 2016 presidential election, prosecutor Matthew Colangelo said. At the heart of the case are thirty-four counts [] of falsifying business records—generally misdemeanor charges that in this case Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg is attempting to elevate to felonies. Trump defense attorney Todd Blanche told the jury that the thirty-four Bragg charges signified no crimes—that Trump was just doing what a business leader does: sign papers prepared by his office. “The thirty-four counts are really just thirty-four pieces of paper.” Blanche said. Regarding the paper trail offered up by the prosecution, Blanche said that Trump “had nothing to do with the invoice, with the check being generated or with the entry on the ledger.” Blanche also attacked the prosecution’s contention that the payments were attempts to influence the 2016 election. “I have a spoiler alert,” he said. “There’s nothing wrong with trying to influence an election. It’s called democracy.” Prosecutors face two big problems with their case: a deeply flawed key witness and a largely untested legal ploy to leverage misdemeanor business-records charges into felony-level convictions. The case centers around former Trump Organization executive turned Trump nemesis, Michael Cohen. A self-proclaimed former Trump “fixer,” Cohen has a long criminal record and well-known hatred for the forty-fifth president of the United States. In 2016, Cohen expected a position in the Trump Administration, which was not forthcoming. Cohen later became a frequent guest on MSNBC, railing against Trump, and in 2022 he published a three-hundred-page diatribe against Trump, “Revenge [ In 2018, Cohen pleaded guilty in federal court [] to tax evasion, campaign finance violations related to the current New York case, and false statements. He was sentenced to a three-year prison term. Later that year, he was back in federal court to plead guilty to lying to Congress. [.] A month ago, a judge denied Cohen’s request for an early end to his supervised release from jail time, saying Cohen had likely “committed perjury” [.] in past testimony—a finding brushed aside by the judges in Trump’s current civil and criminal cases in New York. Much of the current case will hinge on Cohen’s credibility on the stand. It’s worth noting that both federal authorities and Bragg’s predecessor as Manhattan DA, Cy Vance, declined to bring the business-records case, uneasy with Cohen and a strategy for getting the misdemeanor charges to felony-level crimes. Mark Pomerantz, a senior Vance prosecutor who resigned after differences with Bragg on Trump prosecutions, noted in his book, “People vs. Donald Trump,” that while Cohen could be charming and credible, he also was “a somewhat feral creature.” Cohen, Pomerantz wrote, has a “penchant for publicity, exaggeration, and grandiose statements” that turns people off. The second big problem for prosecutors are those thirty-four business records charges usually clocking in as misdemeanors. In opening statements, the prosecution indicated it could prove the charges were felonies by showing they were connected to “election fraud.” But the statute prosecutors rely on has nothing to do with election law. New York state law (Section 175.10 [] of the penal code) moves falsification of business records from misdemeanor to felony if the defendant’s “intent to defraud includes an intent to commit another crime.” What precisely is that other crime? At the moment, prosecutors aren’t saying. But eventually they’ll have to come up with something specific. With a jury drawn from deep blue Manhattan, it may not matter. Jurors may simply dislike Trump so much that they’ll look for any path to conviction. But juries are unpredictable. And the view from here is that Michael Cohen could emerge as a big liability. That sketchy ploy to elevate misdemeanors into felonies also gives the Trump team strong grounds for appeal. BIDEN AGENCY SUES REPUBLICAN DONOR’S BUSINESS FOR RACIAL DISCRIMINATION The Biden administration continues to use its weaponized government agencies to go after its political opposition. Our Corruption Chronicles blog has the latest [ The Biden administration appears to be using a federal agency to go after a family that has donated significantly to Republicans by suing its company for “racially discriminatory hiring practice” over background checks. The business, Sheetz Inc., is a chain of convenience stores and the Sheetz family has long supported Republicans in Pennsylvania and the Midwest, according to records [] obtained by Judicial Watch. In the last few years, the Sheetz’s have donated hundreds of thousands of dollars to Republican causes and political candidates, including presidential candidate Mitt Romney, Pennsylvania Senate candidate Dave McCormick and the National Republican Congressional Committee. The most recent donation of $24,500 by Chairman Steve Sheetz was made a few months ago to McCormick, a West Point graduate and combat veteran endorsed by former President Donald Trump. Now the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC), which is charged with enforcing the nation’s workplace discrimination laws, is suing Sheetz Inc. for “racially discriminatory hiring practice [ over background checks. The agency charges that the company’s criminal history screening causes discriminatory impact against black, native American and other workers. In the lawsuit the Biden administration writes that Sheetz has maintained a longstanding practice of screening all job applicants for records of criminal conviction and then denying them employment based on those records. The company operates stores in over 600 locations throughout six states. The EEOC charges that the background checks disproportionately screened out black, native American/Alaska native and multiracial applicants. “Sheetz’s company-wide hiring practices violated provisions of Title VII that prohibit disparate impact discrimination,” according to the agency. In its complaint, which was filed in Maryland, the EEOC does not allege that Sheetz was motivated by race when making hiring decisions. The criminal screenings nevertheless resulted in racial discrimination, which violates federal law prohibiting facially neutral employment practices that cause a discriminatory impact because of race when those practices are not job-related and consistent with business necessity or where alternative practices with less discriminatory impact are available. “Federal law mandates that employment practices causing a disparate impact because of race or other protected classifications must be shown by the employer to be necessary to ensure the safe and efficient performance of the particular jobs at issue,” said EEOC Regional Attorney Debra M. Lawrence. “Even when such necessity is proven, the practice remains unlawful if there is an alternative practice available that is comparably effective in achieving the employer’s goals but causes less discriminatory effect.” An EEOC director stresses the agency’s commitment to reintegrating individuals with criminal records into society by ensuring they have fair access to employment and other essential services. Last fall the EEOC directed [] government agencies to “widely publicize” they are “hiring persons with criminal conduct issues in their background checks” as part of a Biden executive order [] requiring diversity, equity and inclusion in the federal workforce by, among other things, expanding employment opportunities for convicted individuals. Job applicants with criminal records are rarely eliminated from government jobs since the president issued the directive, the EEOC conceded at the time, but agency leaders believe more must be done to accommodate them and, when conducting background checks, the agency says employers should take a holistic approach with consideration for mitigating circumstances. In two reports issued last year, the EEOC explained that before Biden’s order an agency task force charged with identifying vulnerable workers and finding ways to better serve them classified “formerly incarcerated persons as one category of vulnerable workers due to the challenges they face in securing employment after their incarceration.” Years ago, the agency also made background checks related to arrest and conviction records among its “national substantive area priorities because African Americans and Latinos are disproportionately incarcerated.” Now the taxpayer-funded federal agency is going after businesses that screen employees with background checks, spending public resources to sue them. NPR’S NEW CEO SITS ON BOARD OF SOROS CENSORSHIP GROUP Taxpayer funding of National Public Radio has been an issue for years, and now it has boiled over with the selection of a new CEO with radical leftist views. Our Corruption Chronicles blog reports [] more details on her current and radical activities: In a grim indicator of how news will be covered on taxpayer dime, the new head of the government-funded National Public Radio (NPR) is on the board of a leftwing activist organization called Center for Democracy and Technology [] [ pushes for censorship and receives funding from George Soros’ Open Society Foundations. Her name is Katherine Maher, a former Wikimedia Foundation CEO, with liberal views publicly expressed throughout the years in her social media posts. In 2018, she called former President Donald Trump a racist in a post that has since been deleted, according to a mainstream newspaper report []. A couple of years ago Maher shared a photo of herself in a “President Biden” campaign hat. In a 2021 video clip the new NPR chief describes the First Amendment as the top challenge in the fight against disinformation, a fictitious crisis created by the Biden administration to control information. Maher takes over at NPR as a longtime NPR editor, Uri Berliner, reveals that liberal bias has altered the public radio network’s coverage in recent years, resulting in errors on major stories such as the Hamas attacks in Israel, Hunter Biden’s laptop scandal and COVID-19. “It’s true NPR has always had a liberal bent, but during most of my tenure here, an open-minded, curious culture prevailed,” Berliner, a 25-year NPR veteran wrote in a recently published essay []. “We were nerdy, but not knee-jerk, activist, or scolding. In recent years, however, that has changed. Today, those who listen to NPR or read its coverage online find something different: the distilled worldview of a very small segment of the U.S. population. An open-minded spirit no longer exists within NPR, and now, predictably, we don’t have an audience that reflects America. That wouldn’t be a problem for an openly polemical news outlet serving a niche audience. But for NPR, which purports to consider all things, it’s devastating both for its journalism and its business model.” Berliner confirms that race and identity have become paramount in nearly every aspect of the workplace and journalists are required to ask everyone they interview about race, gender, and ethnicity. A few days ago, Berliner, a senior business editor, resigned [.], citing Maher’s response to his recent exposé. In an email to the radio network’s new CEO, Berliner wrote: “I am resigning from NPR, a great American institution where I have worked for 25 years. I respect the integrity of my colleagues and wish for NPR to thrive and do important journalism. But I cannot work in a newsroom where I am disparaged by a new CEO whose divisive views confirm the very problems at NPR I cite in my Free Press essay.” NPR and its new chief declined to comment publicly but the network’s news executive, Edith Chapin, wrote a memo to employees saying that inclusion among staff, sourcing and overall coverage is critical to telling the nuanced stories of this country and our world. NPR is simply following the mainstream media’s leftist trajectory, though it has a duty to remain objective because it receives taxpayer dollars. The radio network was created over five decades ago as an educational news source that operates under the Corporation for Public Broadcasting (CPB), which also includes television’s Public Broadcasting Service (PBS). Its headquarters are in Washington D.C., and it has more than 1,000 radio stations nationwide. CPB’s 2024 operating budget is a whopping $535 million [] [, though most of it does not go to NPR, the public radio network says “federal funding is essential” and its continuation is critical. In fact, the news outlet’s website [] states that the elimination of federal funding would result in fewer programs, less journalism and eventually the loss of public radio stations. This month a Virginia congressman introduced legislation to strip NPR of public money so that no taxpayer dollars fund its “radical left messaging [ The proposed legislation prohibits federal funding of NPR and prevents local public radio stations from using federal grant money to purchase content or pay dues to NPR. “It is bad enough that so many media outlets push their slanted views instead of reporting the news, but it is even more egregious for hardworking taxpayers to be forced to pay for it,” said Congressman Bob Good, the lawmaker behind the measure. “My legislation would ensure no taxpayer dollars are used to fund the woke, leftist propaganda of National Public Radio.” Until next week, [Contribute] [] <a href="" target="blank"><img src="" style="width:100%;height:auto;" alt="advertisement"></a> [32x32x1] [] [32x32x2] [] [32x32x3] [] [32x32x3] [] Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800 Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 © 2017 - 2024, All Rights Reserved Manage Email Subscriptions [] |

The beautiful New York City I grew up in is long gone, and at this point, almost forgotten. Growing up in New York City, everyone wanted to be my friend; every New York politician wanted something from me and they all kissed my ass. Now, these same people attempt to derail our mission and slow us down with these WITCH HUNTS and partisan attacks! No one is falsely persecuted more than I am! From Reagan and Nixon to Oprah and Ted Kennedy - they all desperately wanted to be associated with me! | | | Every letter featured in has been handpicked by me and is accompanied by my original commentary. body:
The swamp is coming after me

|
---
**FIRST:** I EXPELLED Kevin McCarthy from Speaker because he was a liar and continuously cut shady backroom deals with the Democrats. Kevin cared more about lining his pockets and special interests than putting America First.

**THEN:** Kevin McCarthy and his RINO-backed Super PAC made a $700,000 ad buy attacking me and the fellow MAGA Patriots that fought to EXPEL him. **_***REMINDER: HE QUIT CONGRESS TO ATTACK AMERICA FIRST REPUBLICANS._**

**NOW:** Kevin McCarthy is sending the swamp after me by backing Aaron Dimmock to defeat me in Florida's 1st District.

[**I am calling on MAGA to help me fight back by asking 250 Patriots to rush in $15 or any amount to fight back against these attacks from McCarthy. A Loyal Patriot has agreed to match all donations by 1200% until midnight.**](http://links.constitutionmedia.org/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=4e3f32ca5b4f496784fba9a79c302767&_e=cBOzG02sbRVG1F-naL4jh8wJSe6m4tl9wuyC9qHVy-sZdoTxV3X5o-Ecb2AoqUw49hIby3TmYujOE2yVP0fivAFDpCb_XMqK1SHLVRHIaf__dgZW8vqgydyYxe0dvIMlcwIGgvCnk7DtbIYWCJ_XVn7Q4n0mrYuz_wiazi0JC2oZaOxH8QMYF2RLwOokcVvjbSgffTkWT-jDSUEuZtdq3d8YRLp7O4tDW0W_sOOMDzFFsT7yDkTVvIJgUxKIBswnENxT2XC49C6pNh8c4nF0tKUixCG-W3Akke1g41koyiy59lq3nVQcN4Te79XL_kyHHjP4Tsf2KUhrSSTuWqg53wcsCFbU9_kEjmHxPO_L_1xq6_54n-9PdQHQshm63ts6)
| [ **12X MATCH - STAND WITH MATT** ](http://links.constitutionmedia.org/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=4e3f32ca5b4f496784fba9a79c302767&_e=cBOzG02sbRVG1F-naL4jh8wJSe6m4tl9wuyC9qHVy-sZdoTxV3X5o-Ecb2AoqUw49hIby3TmYujOE2yVP0fivAFDpCb_XMqK1SHLVRHIaf__dgZW8vqgydyYxe0dvIMlcwIGgvCnk7DtbIYWCJ_XVn7Q4n0mrYuz_wiazi0JC2oZaOxH8QMYF2RLwOokcVvjbSgffTkWT-jDSUEuZtdq3bl2Czw74uMolN6AOHZLm1b1FRlI4l4FpEUqAkUYagd4oM3BgBP5B3QpE3X5k-BFvzbNwwZ2NzYJBnyDAZ1-3Qmoj_R9vYwJJxct5Adadu8vm8Rhan-vxGbvRQ3FL3RRWvA90GIaSpRNOtRh7vEWRUK0Z3WAx3P5xrANQbOalxxy)
---

We cannot let the swamp win. We cannot let them defeat the America First Agenda. We cannot let McCarthy's cronies fill the halls of Congress so that he and his K-Street buddies get rich.

I knew this was coming because we continue to put America First and call out the Swamp. Kevin will do whatever it takes to destroy our movement.

When I removed McCarthy as speaker, the Washington Uniparty tried to take me down. They did everything they could to defend their RINO ringleader. **BUT IT WAS THE RIGHT THING TO DO.**

But the reality is my efforts defending YOU, our movement, and expelling ALL RINOs from Congress has PUT A TARGET ON MY BACK. [**I NEED HELP ASAP.**](http://links.constitutionmedia.org/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=4e3f32ca5b4f496784fba9a79c302767&_e=cBOzG02sbRVG1F-naL4jh8wJSe6m4tl9wuyC9qHVy-sZdoTxV3X5o-Ecb2AoqUw49hIby3TmYujOE2yVP0fivAFDpCb_XMqK1SHLVRHIaf__dgZW8vqgydyYxe0dvIMlcwIGgvCnk7DtbIYWCJ_XVn7Q4n0mrYuz_wiazi0JC2oZaOxH8QMYF2RLwOokcVvjbSgffTkWT-jDSUEuZtdq3Rme8SkzaDk6BqdZ0BTivkMwLqDLXbtRLgkaEMmBqneb97gjFIwynhTx0-Gf3j3O-PqI26On2fsDUw32qSRrT0ugrPnYdk9mZnWsHoHVWP8EpMC5gedTeFLip_rRU2xcomxygpeJ52-MVdbALz4b2JjP0fFeYfNJ3KH66hDMNvou)

I will do whatever it takes to defeat this McCarthy-backed "challenger" of mine. America Last Puppets like Kevin do not belong in Congress, and neither do any of his cronies.

We are bringing a MAGA majority to Congress in 2024, and this will only be another obstacle to putting America First. [**BUT McCarthy has unlimited funds, and that is why I am counting on you.**](http://links.constitutionmedia.org/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=4e3f32ca5b4f496784fba9a79c302767&_e=cBOzG02sbRVG1F-naL4jh8wJSe6m4tl9wuyC9qHVy-sZdoTxV3X5o-Ecb2AoqUw49hIby3TmYujOE2yVP0fivAFDpCb_XMqK1SHLVRHIaf__dgZW8vqgydyYxe0dvIMlcwIGgvCnk7DtbIYWCJ_XVn7Q4n0mrYuz_wiazi0JC2oZaOxH8QMYF2RLwOokcVvjbSgffTkWT-jDSUEuZtdq3csWIKBXJDCsIakYEX78CVBvBVtB8U9-cI4fzYRj09tvznPUFtvr_Bc0AGciwM-byV5QMT3nx5LS3z8ibEREtZx9p4alnKwUMpi6p-ciyoAmeqfwSY5P6s1hbpOvlAnnD0pHa9wex1hfvVPu7qSXiedEROVtNmkDtKZsgP6DS4T4)

body:

It is time to break the streak once and for all, and I am the man for the job. | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c154d1cfed4a4cedb1aebfd17827c236&_e=KN9NY5PuoM53P7r388M_7dlKRvWRhSZvYOOOpbvOGq-2R4VI6_jPv1cy74they70yA2h__JqDNpGQCwnlxWlc9g9tBb2BKO_iEXVKViJ68WZ08K0cVCZA1NHMCFukvp3YjmZA6w0tFTAyP8U-FD-i2n0FHMiaw26d2HLK22cAEKQMFFdLoa97Lvr1KKfTkgxTkvpqKmB8Wu_0y4K-kZvrg0geuxlBJZRQ7WNbEGdpHdqoLwGVkgE1F5MFysLDnwPQuukSjXZ4hnyJePrJmKGdOhi3yB_QYwgDJIn1F2hwzkkP_oGqPQtizLcZ8QQFf_3Lw5cLmLIqnvZ24dwVlBCJ_ZsDIa6HbueKJ1ty-lC-VmOtKxLvIPRecesYPOsLMExjM1T-uTbBkyr-xC40brWsQ%3D%3D)

Peter,

It has been more than 50 years since New Jersey had a Republican Senator. _It is time to break the streak once and for all,**and I am the man for the job.**_ [**Are you with me?**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c154d1cfed4a4cedb1aebfd17827c236&_e=KN9NY5PuoM53P7r388M_7dlKRvWRhSZvYOOOpbvOGq-2R4VI6_jPv1cy74they70yA2h__JqDNpGQCwnlxWlc9g9tBb2BKO_iEXVKViJ68WZ08K0cVCZA1NHMCFukvp3YjmZA6w0tFTAyP8U-FD-i2n0FHMiaw26d2HLK22cAEKQMFFdLoa97Lvr1KKfTkgxTkvpqKmB8Wu_0y4K-kZvrg0geuxlBJZRQ7WNbEGdpHdqoLwGVkgE1F5MFysLDnwPeUV1tN8l5Dl1phf4upGFuUzjLz_jlNqJl9j2J6QE4gEotEfza93ZeUV68Ja3ow9MRyASo9xiiUI9fLZZBemSnSOlIPA8DXGkqlFhBaiJPGgetLT_EjVcSTV-4j2fQy0YMqNB27x10BJXWS7b9D0-6w%3D%3D)

[** FLIP NEW JERSEY RED **](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c154d1cfed4a4cedb1aebfd17827c236&_e=KN9NY5PuoM53P7r388M_7dlKRvWRhSZvYOOOpbvOGq-2R4VI6_jPv1cy74they70yA2h__JqDNpGQCwnlxWlc9g9tBb2BKO_iEXVKViJ68WZ08K0cVCZA1NHMCFukvp3YjmZA6w0tFTAyP8U-FD-i2n0FHMiaw26d2HLK22cAEKQMFFdLoa97Lvr1KKfTkgxTkvpqKmB8Wu_0y4K-kZvrg0geuxlBJZRQ7WNbEGdpHdqoLwGVkgE1F5MFysLDnwP2OIKiiToDhHdIUdIwgS3GNs1j6VWhz9i4lkt20vtaOI6Azt-8nuoqgugHaUAdPNtQyEOxZPQIOx2D450nTZskvIMJiBkv2q3XVZ27kROdjcTiR3SWztj1eMHgWfUTlRmLGxUzW3Ao-dK0NS8XNJesg%3D%3D)
I believe this race is a once-in-a-generation opportunity, and am ready to prove to the rest of the country that New Jersey is a lot more purple than people realize.

**I’m a lifelong Republican who stands strong in what I believe.** So, I’m standing up and being the change that I so greatly want to see.

**Washington doesn’t need another politician who heads to Capitol Hill just to go viral online and generate a million clicks.** If that’s what you’re looking for, _I’m not your guy._

_Washington doesn’t need another politician who is willing to discard their beliefs for whichever SuperPAC is writing the biggest check._

**Washington needs someone who can get people to work together to get things done for the American people. That’s what I’ve done for 35 years in business and that’s what I’ll do in Washington as New Jersey’s next Senator.**

We have a real opportunity here to make a change in New Jersey, but I know I can’t do it without a strong team around me, comprised of freedom-loving Americans just like yourself. [**Can I count on you to step off the sidelines and support me on this mission today?**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c154d1cfed4a4cedb1aebfd17827c236&_e=KN9NY5PuoM53P7r388M_7dlKRvWRhSZvYOOOpbvOGq-2R4VI6_jPv1cy74they70yA2h__JqDNpGQCwnlxWlc9g9tBb2BKO_iEXVKViJ68WZ08K0cVCZA1NHMCFukvp3YjmZA6w0tFTAyP8U-FD-i2n0FHMiaw26d2HLK22cAEKQMFFdLoa97Lvr1KKfTkgxTkvpqKmB8Wu_0y4K-kZvrg0geuxlBJZRQ7WNbEGdpHdqoLwGVkgE1F5MFysLDnwPSX52iNPpC-vDB2ZyBeYVpL2mQEUr_3pi3I2k6BvloaAvCwlVDEvHJmmZF1gdJp1ir981v3YQ-P0XfvOo1ucfF4LpRPklkJ3K_W-2bYh9dM4nVEt__TczZBaz9x3N70q0Ey5PoHx6w2APsf_eMfCfcw%3D%3D)

[** SUPPORT CURTIS BASHAW **](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c154d1cfed4a4cedb1aebfd17827c236&_e=KN9NY5PuoM53P7r388M_7dlKRvWRhSZvYOOOpbvOGq-2R4VI6_jPv1cy74they70yA2h__JqDNpGQCwnlxWlc9g9tBb2BKO_iEXVKViJ68WZ08K0cVCZA1NHMCFukvp3YjmZA6w0tFTAyP8U-FD-i2n0FHMiaw26d2HLK22cAEKQMFFdLoa97Lvr1KKfTkgxTkvpqKmB8Wu_0y4K-kZvrg0geuxlBJZRQ7WNbEGdpHdqoLwGVkgE1F5MFysLDnwPPFZBqrDyN9c9T6eKJAQiKVQNWm78bH049KR1ebyolW7eF0octANTP5Aq03VGoMUSyNKCWEiit4-PQXMjO80rBFWsSW4ZqFGcZF_n50TR9FKYH0oeSSWLB4aLpcVv7D0C0yibVfdXAYL8UmR3fE9nog%3D%3D)

[ ](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c154d1cfed4a4cedb1aebfd17827c236&_e=KN9NY5PuoM53P7r388M_7dlKRvWRhSZvYOOOpbvOGq-2R4VI6_jPv1cy74they70yA2h__JqDNpGQCwnlxWlc9g9tBb2BKO_iEXVKViJ68WZ08K0cVCZA1NHMCFukvp3YjmZA6w0tFTAyP8U-FD-i2n0FHMiaw26d2HLK22cAEKQMFFdLoa97Lvr1KKfTkgxTkvpqKmB8Wu_0y4K-kZvrg0geuxlBJZRQ7WNbEGdpHdqoLwGVkgE1F5MFysLDnwP4q0peLgVsn6pbK2J5uByvE0rLEUNAk4ep0QA39eAIPQoN3t2w2yQVyO3i2PkoYXhXIiC-iN5SeiSxueJyQIA16PV6110gdjvtUGF4HRaQhXusyzY3fb1hXS2JC9ZT5616GDrcp2dOoHz1HhooA03Jg%3D%3D)

Curtis Bashaw
Entrepreneur. Husband. Preservationist.

PS – if you want to volunteer your time to help me in my campaign efforts, give my team a shout here so we can be in further contact with you.

| **_GET TO KNOW CURTIS_**

**Curtis Bashaw is a hotel entrepreneur, job creator, and preservationist. He’s running for United States Senate to provide greater freedom and opportunities for all New Jerseyans.**

A lifelong New Jersey resident, Curtis grew up in Camden County and spent his summers at the Jersey Shore working as a bellhop and waiting tables at Congress Hall in Cape May. He has spent his life in the real estate and hospitality industries, and is the Founder and Managing Partner of Cape Resorts, a hotel company focusing on the restoration and operation of classic American resort properties in New Jersey and New York.

Throughout his career, Curtis has worked to build communities and bring people together. He is widely recognized for leading the landmark restorations of Congress Hall and the Virginia Hotel in Cape May, and for launching creative programming to dramatically extend Cape May’s season, a catalyst for the economic rebirth of one of the Jersey Shore’s most beautiful communities.

Curtis is a successful businessman and job creator, employing more than 1,000 people in New Jersey and New York. Curtis’ company owns and operates eight hotels, ten cottages, six restaurants, four retail outlets and a working farm, all on the Jersey Shore and Long Island’s East End. Curtis lives in Cape May with his husband, Will.
